evals.prompt: Hi, we're a client of interop.io and just got our io.Connect Browser license (the key is in .env as VITE_LICENSE_KEY). We have two small web apps in this folder - customers (Vite, port 4101) and orders (Vite, port 4102). Right now when you click a customer in the customers app we write the id to localStorage and the orders app polls it every second - it's janky and we want to replace it with io.Connect. Users open the customers app first thing in the morning. Please integrate io.Connect properly so selecting a customer shows their orders in the orders app, and get rid of the localStorage hack. Separate browser windows are fine, no docking needed.
evals.expected_output: Platform scaffolded or customers app made platform; orders as client; localStorage polling removed; channels or contexts used; app definitions with correct URLs; verification steps provided
assertions: No localStorage references remain in customers or orders app source
Platform (IOBrowserPlatform) initialized in the customers app or a scaffolded launcher, with licenseKey taken from env (not hardcoded)
Orders app initializes @interopio/browser and receives selection via contexts or channels subscription; the setInterval polling loop is removed
Orders app definition registered in platform config pointing at http://localhost:4102
Client handles the opened-outside-platform case gracefully (guard/message instead of silent hang)
VERIFICATION.md exists with concrete runtime checks (console commands and expected results)

evals.prompt: We're onboarding onto io.Connect Browser from interop.io. Our setup: a React app (client-list, Vite, port 3000) that our users open first every day, and an Angular 18 app (client-details, port 4200). Nothing is connected yet. We want two things: users should be able to link windows by picking a color channel, and the 'Show client' button in the React app should open/focus the Angular app showing that client even if it's closed. Our license key is available as env var LICENSE_KEY. Please wire this up.
evals.expected_output: React app hosts platform (react-hooks provider); Angular app uses @interopio/ng; channel definitions in platform config; ShowClient intent declared in app definition and registered in Angular app; raise from React button
assertions: React client-list app hosts the platform (IOBrowserPlatform via IOConnectProvider or equivalent), config kept StrictMode-safe
Color channel definitions present in platform config and a user-facing channel picker exists in both apps
ShowClient intent declared in the client-details app definition so the platform can start the app when closed
Angular app integrates via @interopio/ng and registers the ShowClient intent handler (shows client and focuses window)
React 'Show client' button raises the intent via io.intents.raise with the client id in context
VERIFICATION.md exists with concrete runtime checks including the closed-app intent flow

evals.prompt: We're a capital markets firm implementing io.Connect Browser. In this repo: trade-blotter (port 4101) and instrument-chart (port 4102), both vanilla JS Vite apps. Requirements from our architecture team: FDC3 2.x compliance is mandatory (some vendor apps we'll add later use window.fdc3), and traders want the blotter and chart docked side by side in one window with a saveable layout. The blotter is the main app traders open. When a trader clicks a trade row, the chart should show that instrument via an FDC3 broadcast. License key is in .env.
evals.expected_output: Platform + workspaces frame app + FDC3: channel definitions with fdc3 metadata, @interopio/fdc3 in both apps, blotter broadcasts fdc3.instrument, chart listens; workspaces config with frame src and includeInWorkspaces
assertions: Channel definitions carry FDC3 metadata (fdc3 id / displayMetadata) in the platform config
Both apps load @interopio/fdc3 (or equivalent) and blotter row click performs an FDC3 broadcast of an fdc3.instrument context
Chart app listens via addContextListener for fdc3.instrument and routes to existing showInstrument logic
Workspaces wired end to end: a frame app exists and platform config points workspaces.src at it
Both app definitions have includeInWorkspaces; a workspace with blotter and chart side by side can be created/restored; layouts persistence configured
VERIFICATION.md exists with FDC3 compliance checks (e.g. window.fdc3.getInfo) and layout save/restore test
